description_long: This object is a compact, vintage-style submachine gun with a wooden stock and a metal receiver. The stock is made of dark-stained wood, featuring a simple, robust design with a slight curve for shoulder support. The receiver and barrel assembly are constructed from dark, weathered metal, with the barrel shroud displaying a series of circular cooling holes. A box magazine extends downward from the receiver, and the trigger is housed within a minimal trigger guard. The overall finish shows signs of wear, suggesting extensive use or age, and the design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th-century military firearms.
description: A compact, vintage submachine gun with a wooden stock, metal receiver, perforated barrel shroud, and a box magazine.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full wooden stock, perforated barrel shroud, and box magazine clearly.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular barrel shroud and the alignment of the barrel and magazine.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock and metal receiver, with the magazine and barrel shroud visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden stock and the top of the receiver.
attribute_barrel_type: short cylindrical barrel with perforated metal shroud for cooling
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with a simple, straight profile and slight ergonomic curve
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple notch rear sight with fixed front post
